cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what made Florence Nightingale decide to become a nurse. The options are A through D. First, I need to refer back to the provided text. Let me read through the relevant parts again. The passage says: "As a child she felt that visiting sick people was both a duty and a pleasure. She enjoyed helping them. At last mind was made up. 'I'm going to be a nurse,' she decided." So, her initial motivation seems to be her belief that visiting the sick was a duty and a pleasure, and she enjoyed helping them. That points towards her desire to help the sick. Looking at the options: (B) Her desire to help the sick. That seems to align with the text. Other options: (A) Her father's support. Actually, in the text, her father says, "Nursing isn't the right work for a lady," so he's not supportive, which rules out A. (C) Her education in Germany and France. The text mentions she went to learn nursing there, but that's after she decided to become a nurse. The education was part of her training,
